mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing equity markets to price in recession risks and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5% during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand without monetary tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return differential compared to baseline in walk-forward testing over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving corporate output capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive short-term equity returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration correlates with negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of recovery regime observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 320, it indicates entrenched inflation that forces aggressive monetary tightening and erodes real corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 320 coincides with positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances during inflationary expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment paired with declining housing starts signals a synchronized downturn in labor and housing demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns as recession probabilities increase.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive returns in more than 55% of triggered cases during contraction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms sustained economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive equity returns over the next quarter during stable growth regimes.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5% fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.
